name: Enterprise ICP & Account Segmentation
description: Defining the top 100 target accounts using a rigorous 3-tier segmentation model based on strategic value, workforce size, and compliance risk exposure. Enterprise ABM does not treat all accounts equally — resources are allocated based on tier.
details: Tier 1 (Top 15 "Whales"): Revenue >$1B — Lendlease, CIMIC, John Holland, Multiplex, Downer
Tier 2 (Next 35): Revenue $200M–$1B — Built, Fulton Hogan, BMD, BESIX/Watpac, Kane
Tier 3 (Remaining 50): Large regional builders and construction firms
Focus: Enterprise builders only — architecture firms, certifiers, and industry bodies are a separate future strategy

name: Buying Committee Persona Framework
description: Enterprise deals involve 6–11 stakeholders across the buying committee. Tools™ must map and tailor messaging to each persona within every target account to multi-thread effectively.
details: Quality/Compliance Director (Champion): Standardises compliance, reduces defect rates
Construction/Project Manager (End User): Saves time interpreting codes, mobile-first
COO (Economic Buyer): Reduces rework costs, mitigates legal/insurance risk
Training/Development Manager (Influencer): Accelerates onboarding, interactive CPD
IT/Systems Director (Technical Evaluator): ISO 9001 certified, data sovereignty, Revit integration

name: Account Planning & Intelligence
description: Creating detailed account dossiers for Tier 1 and Tier 2 targets, including recent company news, major project wins, identified stakeholders, and hypotheses on their specific compliance pain points.
details: Weeks 1 to 4 — before any outreach occurs
Involves cleaning CRM data and ensuring tracking mechanisms are in place
Align sales and marketing on the finalised Target Account List (TAL)
Create dossiers with recent news, project wins, and pain point hypotheses

name: Mining Existing Database
description: Identifying current free users or Individual PRO users employed by target Tier 1 and Tier 2 firms to serve as internal champions and first-party intent signals.
targetAudience: Existing Free Users at Target Firms
details: Audit current free users to identify enterprise accounts with organic adoption
Define creative landing pages and establish key CTAs
Implement self-qualification mechanisms
Flag PLG expansion opportunities for Engage phase

name: External Data Enrichment
description: ACTIVATE LATER: Once the internal known-contacts pathway is mastered, employ external data enrichment tools to identify Quality Directors, COOs, and IT Directors not yet in the CRM, completing the buying committee map at each target account.
details: Delay outreach until the target list is fully created
Complete buying committee map for all Tier 1 and Tier 2 accounts
Identify 6–10 key stakeholders at each account
Use tools like ZoomInfo, Apollo, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator

name: High-Value Direct Mail & Executive Gifting
description: Physical pattern interrupts to command executive attention. In an era of overflowing digital inboxes, direct mail serves as a powerful differentiator that becomes a topic of conversation within the target account.
details: "Site Safety Audit" Kit: custom-branded hard hat with prospect's logo + Tools™ logo
Bespoke printed report on their projects + digitised compliance savings
Dimensional mailers (boxes, tubes) — rarely intercepted by gatekeepers
Handwritten note from the Tools™ founder + Personalised URL (PURL)
Glossy brochures with QR codes ("10 Enterprise Playbooks")

name: Omni-channel Content & Email Distribution
description: Flooding the market with high-value thought leadership content AND direct email outreach across multiple channels. Focuses on the cost of defects, NCC complexity, and digital compliance transformation. Content warms the market; direct EDM sequences drive action from known contacts.
details: Direct EDM sequence to known CRM contacts: 3-part personalised email cadence to enterprise accounts in database
LinkedIn posts on the company page — commercial builder pain points, NCC updates, compliance ROI
PR releases and blog republishing on buildingtools.co
Content syndication: whitepapers and case studies through MBA, HIA publications
Thought leadership: cost of building defects, NCC update complexities, defect liability

name: Enterprise LinkedIn Ads & Google Conquesting
description: Running targeted LinkedIn PPC and Google competitor conquesting ads to buying committee personas within the top 100 enterprise accounts. This is the enterprise-specific paid media campaign — separate from the Generic Campaign (Meta + Google) targeting all builder sizes.
details: Primary channel: LinkedIn PPC — precise account and persona targeting, ~$18 CPC (was $60, now more affordable)
Google Search: competitor conquesting ads — "Tools vs. Hammertech", "Procore Alternatives"
Google Display Remarketing: cookie ABM landing page visitors and remarket across display network
LinkedIn requires a completely different strategy vs. Meta/Google — activate after generic campaign proves CPA
Scale trigger: once Generic Campaign attribution is confirmed and budget allows, shift to LinkedIn

name: 3-Part Short Sequence
description: Deploying a concise, impactful email sequence to the curated target list designed to generate initial awareness and interest before the full engagement cadence begins.
details: 3-part email sequence
Concise, impactful messaging with account-specific relevance
Designed for initial awareness — not the full ABM cadence
Timed to land after content warm-up phase (Week 5+)

name: Product-Led Growth Escalation
description: Leveraging existing free-tier usage within enterprise accounts as the ultimate first-party intent data source. Also the primary conversion path for the Generic Campaign — when builders sign up via Meta/Google ads, PLG tracks their trial usage and either converts them to PRO or escalates them to enterprise.
targetAudience: All Trial Users, Tier 1 Builders with Existing Free Users
details: Generic Track: ad click → free trial → PRO subscription ($287/year) → potential enterprise
Enterprise Track: "Hi [CIO], three of your site teams are using Tools™. Can I show you the executive dashboard?"
Create confidential internal case studies using actual product usage data
Show how one division was successful to sell enterprise-wide licence to procurement/IT
Upsell path: Free → Individual PRO → Enterprise

name: Video Prospecting
description: Sales reps recording short, personalised 60-second videos showing the prospect's actual website or a mock-up of their project inside the Tools™ interface. This pattern interrupt is highly effective for executive outreach.
targetAudience: Key Decision Makers at Target Accounts
details: 60-second personalised demo videos per account
Show prospect's actual project inside Tools™ (e.g., Box Gutter compliance)
Use tools like Vidyard or Loom — video emails lift open rates 35%+
Click-through rates increase by over 150% with embedded video

name: Events and Roundtables - Tier 2
description: Targeted events and roundtables for Tier 2 commercial builders. Not conference booths - intimate formats designed to create face-to-face trust and compress the sales cycle with qualified Tier 2 prospects.
targetAudience: Tier 2 Commercial Builders and Industry Leaders
details: Roundtable lunches: 10-20 people, private room, peer networking focus - no booths or large formats
Trial event in Melbourne first, then Sydney - curated invite list of Tier 2 commercial builders
Digital CPD Summit: short 45-minute sessions, educational format with soft sales undertone
FCON-Tech 2026 (Sydney, Oct): pre-event outreach 2 weeks prior, orchestrate 1:1 coffee meetings
Post-event: follow up within 24 hours with conversation-specific messaging from the sales team

name: Layered Targeting & Social Selling
description: Moving beyond job titles to target by function and seniority with a hyper-personalised warm-up sequence. Upload the verified top 100 account list directly into LinkedIn Campaign Manager for precision targeting.
details: Competitor conquesting ads: comparison-focused (Tools™ vs. Hammertech)
Warm-up sequence: Engage content → Connect → Follow up with value, not pitches
Day +1/+2/+5 post-connection with high-value assets (ROI calculator)
Layer targeting: C-Suite (ROI), Operations/HSE (compliance), IT/Procurement (security)

name: Intent-Driven Email Cadence
description: Deliberately multi-threading to three or more roles across the buying committee simultaneously. True ABM email requires account-specific relevance in the very first line — if a template can be sent to 500 people without alteration, it is not ABM.
details: Day 1 (Hook): Specific account trigger — e.g., recent project win, compliance news
Day 4 (Proof): Embedded personalised video via Vidyard/Loom
Day 7 (Value): Relevant case study — Hutchinson Builders or Richard Crookes
Day 12 (Breakup): Polite withdrawal, leaves door open

name: 1:1 and 1:Few Web Experiences
description: Custom-built landing pages for each target account. Generic homepages do not convert enterprise buyers — they should land on a page built specifically for them, featuring their logo, tailored messaging, and relevant case studies.
details: 1:1 pages built per account: "How Hickory Can Digitise WHS Compliance Across Active Sites"
Feature prospect's logo, pre-filled ROI calculator, direct calendar link to AE
1:Few pages for verticals: Civil Engineering vs. Commercial High-Rise
Serve case studies and compliance features specific to each sub-sector
exampleLabel: View Example: Hickory ABM Landing Page

name: Initial Sales Process
description: Deep-diving into compliance challenges and presenting the tailored Tools™ solution to qualified prospects, supported by the full arsenal of enterprise sales collateral.
details: Supported by Enterprise Pitch Decks
Implementation guides and use cases
Custom pricing models
Leverage visual product demonstrations as the "aha moment"

name: Weaponising Existing Assets
description: Tools™ has unique competitive advantages that must be systematically deployed across all ABM touchpoints — endorsements from Tier 1 clients, certifications, patents, and the CPD partnership.
details: Hutchinson Builders & Richard Crookes endorsements — strongest social proof available
Visual product demos: "A picture tells 1,000 words" — show complex NCC simplified into one graphic
ISO 9001 Certification & Patents: enterprise-grade credibility for risk-averse procurement
CPD/PointsBuild partnership: enterprise firms mandated to provide CPD, Tools™ solves this

name: Customised Proposals & Enablement
description: Once an opportunity is created, marketing supports sales to accelerate the deal with tailored business cases and enablement resources for the internal champion.
details: Develop tailored business cases / ROI models for the specific account
Stakeholder enablement: security docs for IT, training overviews for HR
Provide champion with resources to sell Tools™ to the rest of the buying committee
Facilitate executive alignment: peer-to-peer meetings between leadership teams

name: Final Sales Process
description: Presenting the formal proposal and securing the enterprise contract, leveraging the full weight of Tier 1 endorsements and proven ROI.
details: Supported by Proposal Decks
Standardised contracts
Relevant success stories: Hutchinson Builders, Richard Crookes references
Tier 1 goal: secure pilot programs on major flagship projects

name: Routing & Immediate Value
description: Handling outcomes based on self-qualification, routing prospects to the appropriate conversion path. Generic Campaign users self-serve to PRO; Enterprise prospects book a demo and enter the sales process.
details: Generic Track: "Buy Now" self-serve PRO subscription ($287/year) — target 10.45 new PROs/day
Enterprise Track: "Book A Demo" → enters enterprise sales process
Receive immediate value: 10 Enterprise Playbooks
Qualify for 30 Days Complimentary PRO Access
30-Day Quick Win pathway: structured onboarding milestones to achieve measurable value fast — change inertia (not price) is the #1 adoption barrier
First 7 days: one team, one tool type, first compliance query answered. First 30 days: first site audit completed digitally

name: Account-Centric Metrics Framework
description: Traditional lead-gen metrics (cost per lead, total traffic) are insufficient for enterprise ABM. Both campaigns require distinct KPI frameworks. Generic Campaign: PRO conversion rate, CPA (trial ~$8, PRO TBD pending attribution fix). Enterprise: account engagement, pipeline value, deal velocity.
details: Generic Campaign KPIs: PRO conversions/day (target: 10.45), CPA trial start (~$8), CPA PRO conversion (TBD — attribution fix required)
Generic Campaign budget: ~$1K/month now — scale to $3–5K/month once CPA confirmed
Enterprise KPIs: MQAs (marketing validated accounts), SQOs, pipeline value, conversion rate, sales cycle length
Account Engagement: % of target accounts engaged, stakeholders engaged per account
Deal Velocity: Average time from first engagement to closed-won (ABM should shorten this)
Revenue & ROI: Enterprise ARR generated, expansion revenue, overall ABM program ROI

name: Meta Ads — Generic Campaign
description: Broad-reach Facebook and Instagram advertising targeting builders of all sizes across Australia. Unlike the Enterprise ABM plan, the Generic Campaign does not restrict to the top 100 accounts — it casts a wide net to drive trial starts and PRO conversions at scale. Purpose: hit Jerry's revenue target of 10.45 new PRO subscribers per day.
targetAudience: All Australian Builders — Small, Medium & Large
details: Target: 10.45 new PRO conversions/day ($3K/day annualised at $287/year)
Current monthly budget: ~$1,000 (very limited — scale once attribution is confirmed)
Current CPA trial start: ~$8 — strong signal, good baseline
CPA PRO conversion: TBD — awaiting attribution fix (dev priority, Buck/Michelle tracking)
Scale model: confirm CPA → reinvest revenue → scale to $3–5K/month → prove → $300K/month vision
Creative: use best-performing existing ads as baseline, test construction compliance hooks
Explore Google payment terms via Ari's contacts to ease cash flow constraints on ad spend

name: Google Ads — Generic Campaign
description: Google Search and Display advertising capturing builders actively searching for compliance tools, NCC updates, and building code software. Runs in parallel with Meta as the second primary channel in the Generic Campaign. LinkedIn is excluded from the generic campaign — its CPC (~$18) makes it too expensive at current budget and it requires an entirely different strategy.
targetAudience: Builders Actively Searching for Compliance Solutions
details: Search campaigns: builders searching for NCC compliance tools, building code software, Procore/Hammertech alternatives
Display remarketing: cookie website visitors and trial starters across Google Display Network
Current CPA trial: ~$8 (combined with Meta — exact per-channel split TBD with attribution)
LinkedIn excluded from generic campaign — reserved for Enterprise ABM once budget allows
Priority: scale Meta + Google first, prove CPA, then use that revenue to fund LinkedIn enterprise ads
Ari is exploring Google payment terms directly — could allow spending ahead of revenue to accelerate scale
Jerry metric: $3K/day annualised revenue = 10.45 new PRO subs/day at $287/year

name: Intent Signal Monitoring
description: Leadfeeder identifies companies visiting buildingtools.co before they make contact. It tracks which enterprise accounts are showing interest, which pages they are browsing and how many staff from each firm are active - giving sales a warm signal to prioritise outreach.
details: Identifies companies by IP address - no form fill required from the prospect
Filters for enterprise-grade accounts matching our ICP criteria
Shows page-level engagement: pricing, features, case studies
Alerts the sales team in real time when a Tier 1 or Tier 2 account is active
Integrates with HubSpot to log visits against existing contact and company records

name: Whale ABM LinkedIn Campaign
description: Hyper-targeted LinkedIn ads directed at Tier 1 enterprise builder executives: Lendlease, CIMIC, Multiplex, John Holland and Downer. Each ad is tailored to the specific role - CIO, CPO, HSE Director and site leadership - with copy focused on compliance liability and defect cost reduction.
targetAudience: C-Suite and Senior Management at Tier 1 (Whale) Builders
details: Persona-specific creative: CIO (technology risk), CPO (procurement ROI), HSE Director (site compliance)
Account-list targeting: upload the Tier 1 company list directly into LinkedIn Campaign Manager
Sequential messaging: awareness ad first, then retargeting with social proof (Hutchinson, Hickory)
Retarget LinkedIn profile visitors and engagers from the ABM account list
Budget ring-fenced for Tier 1 only - not blended with generic campaign spend

name: Whale Lumpy Mailout for Tier 1
description: Physical pattern interrupts designed to cut through the noise at the C-suite level. Each piece is personalised to the target firm and their current projects, accompanied by a handwritten note from the Tools founder.
details: Custom enterprise brochure tailored to the target account and their project portfolio
Handwritten founder note from Jerry Tyrrell - personal, direct and hard to ignore
Dimensional mailer format to avoid being intercepted by admin gatekeepers
Personalised URL (PURL) on the brochure for tracking and attribution
Followed immediately by a phone call - the mail gives the sales team a reason to call

name: Whale-Specific Content and EDM
description: Bespoke content pieces and direct email sequences built specifically for Tier 1 enterprise builders. Unlike the generic broad-reach content, whale content is personalised to the target firm and addresses their specific compliance pain points.
targetAudience: Quality Managers and C-Suite at Tier 1 Accounts
details: Hickory-branded whitepaper: how Tools helps Tier 1 builders manage NCC compliance across active sites
Custom flyer referencing the target account's active project portfolio and NCC requirements
3-part personalised EDM sequence triggered after the direct mail lands
Content written specifically for commercial construction - not residential or architects
Each piece positions Tools as essential compliance infrastructure, not optional software

name: Whale Personalised Video Prospecting
description: Short 60-second personalised videos recorded specifically for each Tier 1 target account. The video shows the prospect their own project inside the Tools platform - making the value immediately tangible.
targetAudience: Quality Manager and CIO at Tier 1 Target Accounts
details: 60-second screen recording showing the prospect's actual active project inside Tools
Script tailored to the target's role: QM version focuses on NCC query resolution, CIO version focuses on enterprise rollout
Sent via Vidyard or Loom - personalised video email lifts open and reply rates significantly
References a known project win or compliance event specific to the target firm
Personalised subject line: 'I made this for [Company] - 60 seconds'

name: Whale LinkedIn ABM Playbook
description: A structured LinkedIn outreach and InMail playbook built specifically for Tier 1 enterprise builders. Goes beyond job-title targeting to approach buying committee members with account-specific relevance and warm-up sequencing.
targetAudience: Buying Committee at Tier 1 Accounts via LinkedIn
details: Account-list upload: verified Tier 1 accounts uploaded directly into LinkedIn Campaign Manager
InMail sequence: Day 1 connection request with personalised note, Day 3 value share, Day 7 case study, Day 14 soft ask
Warm-up sequence: engage with their posts before outreach to build recognition
Multi-threading: simultaneously target QM, CIO and COO at the same account
Content to share: Hickory case study, NCC update commentary and ROI calculator

name: Generic EDM Campaign
description: Direct email broadcast to the full marketing database. Runs alongside Meta and Google as a live generic channel - driving trial registrations and PRO conversions through automated EDM sequences. Not account-specific; designed for volume and brand awareness.
details: Automated EDM sequences triggered by sign-up and engagement behaviour
Nurture sequence: trial start to PRO conversion
Broadcast to general builder audience - not ABM personalised
Runs in parallel with Meta and Google as a continuous live channel
Connects directly to Conversion Outcomes (PRO subscription or Book A Demo)

name: Tier 1 Whale VIP Event Strategy
description: Exclusive, intimate VIP experiences designed specifically for Tier 1 whale accounts. Curated roundtable lunches and private dinners engineered so that target executives see their exact industry peers at the table - making the conversation about adoption, not evaluation.
targetAudience: C-Suite and Quality Managers at Tier 1 (Whale) Accounts
details: Intimate roundtable lunches: 10-15 people maximum, invite-only, peer-curated attendee list
VIP dinners hosted by Jerry Tyrrell and Siva Gounder - relationship-first, no hard sell at the event
Invite list engineered: known Tier 1 whale contacts plus peer accounts to create FOMO and social proof
Location: premium venues in Sydney and Melbourne near Lendlease, CIMIC and Multiplex headquarters
When a Tier 1 QM sees their peers at the table, the conversation shifts from 'should we try it' to 'when do we start'

name: Whale EDM Outreach Sequence
description: A personalised, account-specific email sequence deployed to Tier 1 whale targets. Unlike the generic EDM broadcast, each whale EDM references the target firm by name, mentions their active projects, and uses named peers (Hutchinson, Hickory, Richard Crookes) as proof. The sequence is designed to get a reply, not just an open.
targetAudience: Quality Managers and C-Suite at Tier 1 (Whale) Accounts
details: 3-part personalised sequence - each email reads as if written specifically for that account
Email 1: account-specific hook referencing a known project, compliance event or industry news
Email 2: proof email - named peers (Hickory, Hutchinson, Richard Crookes) who adopted the platform
Email 3: professional close - polite, leaves the door open without being pushy
Triggered after the direct mail lands - mail gives sales a warm reason to follow up digitally
